contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Credential vault — migrated to own public repo + LLM-runnable governor onboarding flow
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/251
  
  Details:
  Gary challenged the V1 placement: 'are you sure we should have this so deeply buried in agentic_ai_context and not its own repo?' Yes — disaster recovery should be the FIRST repo cloned on a fresh Mac.
  
  Created TrueSightDAO/credential_vault (public, hardened .gitignore against any commit of *.age / *.json / .env* / *_rsa / *secret* / *token* / *credential* / *passphrase*). Migrated all V1 content (MANIFEST.txt, backup.sh, restore.sh, threat model) and added two new pieces aligned to Gary's vision:
  
  1. ONBOARDING.md — LLM-runnable step-by-step for new governor architects. Steps 0-10 cover toolchain, repo clones, DAO RSA key, gh auth, clasp per-account, Google SA keys, SSH, AWS, project .env files, manifest customization, and final vault setup. Each step has confirmation checkpoints.
  
  2. Local progress tracking — LLM creates ~/.credential_vault_onboarding_progress.md (per-laptop, uncommitted, 0600) so the new governor can resume across sessions with 'what do I do next?'. The doc specifies the template + update protocol.
  
  3. Permission escalation — when she hits a step requiring access she doesn't have (clasp not granted on a script, SA key not shared, IAM user not provisioned), the LLM generates a copy-pasteable request she sends to her sponsoring governor. Template + detection triggers documented.
  
  agentic_ai_context updated:
  - Stripped inline vault content
  - CREDENTIAL_VAULT.md collapsed to a redirect with explicit LLM routing rules ('when user mentions new governor / lost my Mac / how do I back up credentials → route to TrueSightDAO/credential_vault')
  - GOOGLE_API_CREDENTIALS.md updated: that doc stays the source of truth for *what* each credential is for; the new repo is the source of truth for *how* to store/restore.
  
  launchd plist on laptop updated to point at new script paths; reload smoke-tested. Memory pointer updated. End-to-end smoke verified: 26 paths → 245 KB snapshot → 268 archive entries decrypted successfully.
  
  Also flipped the new repo PUBLIC after Gary's follow-up — original private was overcautious (same pattern as krake_browser 2026-05-20). Hardened .gitignore now belt-and-suspenders against accidental credential commits.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
